title: Projection is not used when query includes filter on a subcolumn
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
When querying a MergeTree table with a projection that orders by `company_name`, a simple filter on `company_name` correctly uses the projection and primary key to prune data efficiently.
However, when adding an additional filter on an `Array(JSON)` column (e.g., `has(locations.country_code, 'US')`), the query planner stops using the projection and primary key condition, even though the `company_name` predicate remains unchanged. This causes the query to scan all granules/parts instead of using the projection for efficient filtering and be potentially much slower.
This appears to be a query planner bug where the presence of a JSON column filter prevents the optimizer from using projections and primary key indexes for other predicates in the same query.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Version used: 25.11.1.558. It also happens in the latest version.
Fiddle: https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/0e3db939-5fb0-436d-b0a6-b9583192cd48
```sql
-- 1. Inspect schema of the source Parquet
DESCRIBE TABLE s3('https://media.theirstack.com/jobs/jan-2025-sample-with-locations.parquet');
-- schema:
-- id            UInt32
-- posted_at     UInt16
-- discovered_at DateTime64(3, 'UTC')
-- url           String
-- title         String
-- company_name  String
-- locations     Array(JSON)
-- 2. Create a MergeTree table with a projection and a JSON array column
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS job_sample;
CREATE TABLE job_sample
(
`id` UInt32,
`posted_at` Date,
`discovered_at` DateTime64(3, 'UTC'),
`url` String,
`title` String,
`company_name` String,
`description` String,
`locations` Array(JSON),
PROJECTION sorted_by_company_name
(
SELECT *
ORDER BY
company_name,
posted_at
)
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(posted_at)
ORDER BY (posted_at, discovered_at)
SETTINGS index_granularity = 128;
-- 3. Load sample data from S3 Parquet
INSERT INTO job_sample
SELECT *
FROM s3('https://media.theirstack.com/jobs/jan-2025-sample-with-locations.parquet', 'Parquet');
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE job_sample_v2 (id INT UNSIGNED, posted_at DATE, discovered_at DATETIME(3), url VARCHAR(255), title VARCHAR(255), company_name VARCHAR(255), description TEXT, locations JSON);

INSERT INTO job_sample_v2 (id, posted_at, discovered_at, url, title, company_name, description, locations) VALUES (1, '2023-01-01', '2023-01-01 12:00:00', 'http://example.com', 'Job Title 1', 'Company A', 'Description A', JSON_ARRAY(JSON_OBJECT('country_code', 'US')));

INSERT INTO job_sample_v2 (id, posted_at, discovered_at, url, title, company_name, description, locations) VALUES (2, '2023-01-02', '2023-01-02 12:00:00', 'http://example.com', 'Job Title 2', 'Company B', 'Description B', JSON_ARRAY(JSON_OBJECT('country_code', 'CA')));
SELECT * FROM job_sample_v2 WHERE company_name = 'Company A' AND JSON_CONTAINS(locations, JSON_OBJECT('country_code', 'US'));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM job_sample_v2 WHERE company_name = 'Company B' AND (JSON_CONTAINS(locations, JSON_OBJECT('country_code', 'US')) OR posted_at < '2023-01-02');
key_bug_features: Filter on Array(JSON) column disables projection usage
